hardware: RTX 3050 4GB VRAM, 20/49 layers on GPU (3901 MiB), rest CPU (24GB RAM)

results.name: B1_apt_intrusion
results.notes: All expected IOCs found: 203.0.113.42, svc_exchange, CORP-DC-01, MD5 hash. No special token leaks.

results.name: B2_lolbin
results.error: json.loads parse error in generated code — code tried to parse alert data with json.loads but string wasn't valid JSON
results.notes: Code generated cleanly (no token leaks) but had a runtime JSON parsing bug.

results.name: B3_firmware
results.notes: All 5 expected IOCs found including both IPs, hostname, hash, and username. Overly broad regex caused 125 total IOC matches (many false positives from username pattern).

results.name: B4_ssh_brute
results.notes: Found 10.0.0.99 and admin. Missed 10.0.0.5 (destination IP) and WEB-PROD-01 (hostname). Duplicate IOCs in output.

results.name: B5_pth
results.notes: All 5 expected IOCs found: both IPs, svc_backup, NTLM hash, mimikatz.exe. Clean code, no token leaks.

conclusion: qwen2.5:14b via llama.cpp is a massive improvement over deepseek-coder:6.7b. 80% execution rate (vs 40%), 89% IOC rate on completed (vs 57%), zero special token leaks (vs 2/5). GPU upgrade to 8GB+ VRAM is justified — would reduce inference from ~12 min to ~2 min per investigation. Path B is viable for production with this model quality.
